input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML (`ItemsPage.xaml`)**

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.ItemsPage"
             xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:MyApp.ViewModels">

    <ContentPage.BindingContext>
        <vm:ItemsViewModel />
    </ContentPage.BindingContext>

    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}"
                    SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding ItemSelectedCommand}"
                    IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
                    RemainingItemsThreshold="0">

        <CollectionView.Header>
            <RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
                         IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">
            </RefreshView>
        </CollectionView.Header>

    </CollectionView>

</ContentPage>
```

Actually — `CollectionView` doesn't directly support pull-to-refresh. You need to **wrap it in a `RefreshView`**:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.ItemsPage"
             xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:MyApp.ViewModels">

    <ContentPage.BindingContext>
        <vm:ItemsViewModel />
    </ContentPage.BindingContext>

    <RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
                 IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">

        <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                        SelectionMode="Single"
                        SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}"
                        SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding ItemSelectedCommand}">
            <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
                <DataTemplate>
                    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="10">
                        <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="18" />
                        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" FontSize="14"
                               TextColor="Gray" />
                    </VerticalStackLayout>
                </DataTemplate>
            </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
        </CollectionView>

    </RefreshView>

</ContentPage>
```

**ViewModel (`ItemsViewModel.cs`)**

```csharp
public partial class ItemsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IMyApiService _api;
    private readonly INavigationService _nav;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<Item> _items = new();

    [ObservableProperty]
    private Item? _selectedItem;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool _isRefreshing;

    public ItemsViewModel(IMyApiService api, INavigationService nav)
    {
        _api = api;
        _nav = nav;
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task Refresh()
    {
        try
        {
            var data = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
            Items = new ObservableCollection<Item>(data);
        }
        finally
        {
            IsRefreshing = false;   // stops the spinner
        }
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task ItemSelected()
    {
        if (SelectedItem is null) return;

        await _nav.GoToAsync(nameof(DetailPage),
            new Dictionary<string, object> { { "Item", SelectedItem } });

        SelectedItem = null;  // reset so the same item can be re-selected
    }
}
```

**Key bindings explained:**

| XAML Property | ViewModel Member | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `ItemsSource` | `ObservableCollection<Item> Items` | Drives the list |
| `SelectionMode="Single"` | — | Allows exactly one selection |
| `SelectedItem` (TwoWay) | `Item? SelectedItem` | Tracks which item is tapped |
| `SelectionChangedCommand` | `ItemSelectedCommand` | Fires navigation on tap |
| `RefreshView.Command` | `RefreshCommand` | Fires API reload on pull |
| `RefreshView.IsRefreshing` | `bool IsRefreshing` | Controls the spinner; **you must set it to `false`** when done |

**Important details:**
- **`RefreshView` wraps `CollectionView`** — `CollectionView` has no built-in pull-to-refresh.
- **Reset `SelectedItem` to `null`** after navigating, otherwise tapping the same item again won't fire the command.
- **Set `IsRefreshing = false`** in a `finally` block so the spinner always stops, even on errors.
- This uses **CommunityToolkit.Mvvm** source generators (`[ObservableProperty]`, `[RelayCommand]`).
